title: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) Boom Could Send the S&P 500 50% Higher by 2025, According to Wall Street
text: Strategists at Capital Economics expect the S&P 500 to climb 50% by the end of 2025, buoyed by excitement surrounding AI. Ark Invest thinks AI software revenue could grow at 42% annually through 2030, creating tailwinds for businesses like Amazon and Datadog.

title: Datadog: Optimism May Not Be Warranted
text: Datadog stock has rebounded significantly over the past two months, but the company continues to operate in a challenging macro environment. Datadog continues to invest aggressively in R&D to develop its platform, which should be supportive of growth when the demand environment improves. DDOG is now fairly expensive given the company's deteriorating growth.

title: Datadog: The Growth Engine Of The Observability Cloud Era
text: Leading B2B SaaS observability player with a revenue CAGR of 66% since FY17 and net revenue retention of 130%. Growing market dominance in large TAM markets, strong track record of product innovation and improving economics. Datadog's valuation is rich, but its forward PEG is expected to decline to more reasonable levels in the next few years, making it a good long-term buy.

title: 7 Growth Stocks to Buy With Explosive Upside Potential
text: Investors find themselves in a landscape transformed by persistent inflation and a banking sector crisis that has rattled most publicly traded firms. However, amidst the chaos, a group of resilient contenders has emerged, representing the top growth stocks to buy.

title: Is Datadog (DDOG) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
text: Investors often turn to recommendations made by Wall Street analysts before making a Buy, Sell, or Hold decision about a stock. While media reports about rating changes by these brokerage-firm employed (or sell-side) analysts often affect a stock's price, do they really matter?

title: A Bull Market Is Coming: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Stocks Down 41% and 51% to Buy Now
text: Artificial intelligence could add $13 trillion to global economic output by 2030. Datadog is a leader in several observability software verticals but the company has hardly scratched the surface of its addressable market.

title: Datadog Announces Annual DASH Conference Set for San Francisco in August
text: Speakers from Google, AWS, Airbnb, NTT DOCOMO and more will discuss how SREs, DevOps teams, developers, security engineers and architects can use observability to positively impact their business NEW YORK , June 13, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Datadog , Inc. (NASDAQ: DDOG), the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications, today announced the details of DASH , its annual conference. The conference will take place on Wednesday, August 2nd and Thursday, August 3rd at Moscone West in San Francisco, California.

title: Datadog: Market Leader In Cloud Observability
text: Datadog competes with Splunk and Dynatrace in the software observability space, a fast-growing SaaS market. Looking at DDOG's relative financials, we see that Datadog has the best balance sheet of the bunch while also being well ahead of the pack in terms of growth. While its valuation reflects this, its shares are not as expensive as they may appear and constitute a good buy for long-term investors.

title: Datadog (DDOG) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
text: From a technical perspective, Datadog (DDOG) is looking like an interesting pick, as it just reached a key level of support. DDOG recently overtook the 20-day moving average, and this suggests a short-term bullish trend.

title: Datadog (DDOG)'s Technical Outlook is Bright After Key Golden Cross
text: After reaching an important support level, Datadog, Inc. (DDOG) could be a good stock pick from a technical perspective. DDOG recently experienced a "golden cross" event, which saw its 50-day simple moving average breaking out above its 200-day simple moving average.

title: Datadog Extends Cloud Cost Management Capabilities with Container Cost Allocation, Cost Monitors and Support for Microsoft Azure
text: New features help customers create a culture of cost ownership across their organization by empowering engineers to understand and take action on cloud costs NEW YORK , June 7, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Datadog , Inc. (NASDAQ: DDOG), the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications, today announced new capabilities for its Cloud Cost Management product, including container cost allocation, cost monitors and support for Microsoft Azure . Cloud Cost Management shows an organization's granular cost data, scoped to specific services, so that engineers can optimize cloud spend and performance.

title: Ably Announces New Integration with Datadog to Streamline
text: LONDON, June 07, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Ably, a Realtime Experience Infrastructure provider, today announced a new integration with Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG), the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications. The integration will enable Ably Enterprise customers who are also Datadog customers to seamlessly monitor their realtime metrics in one place to ensure their applications […]...

title: Datadog Launches Workflow Automation for Faster Remediation of Application Issues
text: Workflow Automation provides customers with out-of-the-box actions and pre-built templates to proactively remediate issues and avoid service disruptions or impact to end users NEW YORK , June 5, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Datadog , Inc. (NASDAQ: DDOG), the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications, today announced the general availability of Workflow Automation. The new product enables teams to automate end-to-end remediation processes—with out-of-the-box actions and pre-built templates—across all systems, apps and services to help identify, investigate and resolve service disruptions and security threats faster.

title: 2 Super Stocks to Buy Hand Over Fist in June

title: 5 Stocks That Powered a Historic May for Nasdaq ETF
text: The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite Index wrapped up a historic month, outperforming the Dow Jones by the widest margin since October 2001.
